question: According to J.B.S. Haldane, the 'hot dilute soup' is made up of ____  [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: According to J.B.S. Haldane, the 'hot dilute soup' is made up of ____  [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. colloidal aggregation of hydrophobic proteins
B. colloidal hydrophilic complex of protein surrounded by water molecules
D. simple organic molecules at the bottom of water bodies
answer: D. simple organic molecules at the bottom of water bodies
solution: According to J.B.S. Haldane, the 'hot dilute soup' is made up of simple organic molecules at the bottom of water bodies.

question: Which one of the following is NOT the main postulate of Darwinism? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which one of the following is NOT the main postulate of Darwinism? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Darwin explained survival of fittest but was not able to explain Arrival of the fittest.

question: Which is the correct sequence in evolutionary history of man from ancient to recent epochs? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which is the correct sequence in evolutionary history of man from ancient to recent epochs? [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Ramapithecus → Dryopithecus → Homo habilis → Homo erectus → Neanderthal man
B. Dryopithecus → Ramapithecus → Homo erectus → Homo habilis → Neanderthal man
C. Ramapithecus → Dryopithecus → Homo erectus → Homo habilis → Neanderthal man
D. Dryopithecus → Ramapithecus → Homo habilis → Homo habilis → Neanderthal man
answer: D. Dryopithecus → Ramapithecus → Homo habilis → Homo habilis → Neanderthal man
solution: Correct sequence in evolutionary history of man from ancient to recent epochs is: Dryopithecus → Ramapithecus → Homo habilis → Homo habilis → Neanderthal man → Modern man.

question: Which one is linked to evolution?
question_latex: Which one is linked to evolution?
solution: Without variations, changes could not occur and there will be no possibility of evolution. Certain variations, which once appeared in the parent generation, continue to appear in the progeny generation after generation.

question: Select the INCORRECT statement with respect to vestigial organs. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Select the INCORRECT statement with respect to vestigial organs. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Presence of vestigial organs provide evidence that man has evolved
B. They are also called as rudimentary organs
D. Presence of nictitating membrane in human is one example
solution: Vestigial organs are imperfectly developed and non-functional, degenerate structures which were functional in some related and other animals or in ancestors.

question: Which one of the following is NOT a common character of apes and human beings?
question_latex: Which one of the following is NOT a common character of apes and human beings?
options: D. Forelimbs shorter than hind limbs
answer: D. Forelimbs shorter than hind limbs
solution: In apes, forelimbs are longer than hind limbs so they almost touch the ground when they walk. In man, hind limbs are longer than fore limbs, helping in maintaining erect posture.

question: In which era, life was absent?
question_latex: In which era, life was absent?
solution: The period of approximately 0.6 billion years that existed between the formation of the gaseous clouds (about 4.6 billion years ago) and the formation of earth's crust is called Azoic era, during which no life existed.

question: Match Column - I with Column - II and select the correct option.
question_latex: Match Column - I with Column - II and select the correct option.
solution: Seymouria is connecting link between amphibians and Reptiles. Ichthyostega is connecting link between fish and amphibian. Archaeopteryx is connecting link between reptiles and birds. Australopithecus is connecting link between Ape and Man.

question: Foot prints are ____ type of fossils. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Foot prints are \_\_\_\_ type of fossils. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Moulds are hardened encasements formed in outer parts of organic remains.

question: Observe the following diagram and select the correct chromosomal aberration from the list given below. ABCDE FGH → MNOCDE FGH [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Observe the following diagram and select the correct chromosomal aberration from the list given below. $ABCDE\ FGH \rightarrow MNOCDE\ FGH$ [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Translocation is the transfer (transposition) of a part of a chromosome or a set of genes to a non-homologous chromosome. It is effected naturally by the transposons present in the cell.

question: Identify the correct sequence of epochs from ancient to recent in geological time scale. Holocene = p, Pleistocene = q, Pliocene = r, Miocene = s, Oligocene = t. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Identify the correct sequence of epochs from ancient to recent in geological time scale. Holocene $= p$, Pleistocene $= q$, Pliocene $= r$, Miocene $= s$, Oligocene $= t$. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. t → s → q → r → p
B. p → q → s → t → r
C. p → q → r → t → s
D. t → s → r → q → p
answer: D. t → s → r → q → p
solution: Oligocene → Miocene → Pliocene → Pleistocene → Holocene is the correct sequence of epochs from ancient to recent in geological time scale.

question: Seymouria is a connecting link between __________ [MHT-CET 2017]
question_latex: Seymouria is a connecting link between \_\_\_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2017]
solution: Seymouria is a connecting link between Amphibians and Reptiles.

question: Every cell of the body contributes gemmules to the germ cells and so shares in the transmission of inherited characters. This theory is known as
question_latex: Every cell of the body contributes gemmules to the germ cells and so shares in the transmission of inherited characters. This theory is known as
options: A. Theory of inheritance of acquired characters
solution: Charles Darwin (1809-1882) tried to suggest the physical basis of heredity by pangenesis theory and suggested that every cell of the body contributes gemmules to the germ cells and so shares in the transmission of inherited characters.

question: Fossilized faecal material of animals are known as
question_latex: Fossilized faecal material of animals are known as
solution: Coprolite is a scientific name for the fossilized excrement, faeces or droppings of ancient animals. It was coined by Dr. William Buckland (1829).

question: Connecting link between amphibians and reptiles is ____ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Connecting link between amphibians and reptiles is \_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Connecting links (missing links) are fossil forms transitional or intermediate between two groups of organisms. Seymouria is the connecting link between amphibians and reptiles, and Archaeopteryx is the connecting link between reptiles and birds.

question: One of the following statements is INCORRECT regarding graphical representation of type of natural selection shown below. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: One of the following statements is INCORRECT regarding graphical representation of type of natural selection shown below. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. It represents genetically stabilizing selection where population is adapted to its environment
B. It results in an evolutionary trend within a population
C. One of the extremes of phenotypic range is eliminated
D. In this type more individuals acquired value other than the mean character value
answer: A. It represents genetically stabilizing selection where population is adapted to its environment
solution: The graph shown is an example of directional selection, not stabilizing selection. It represents genetically directional selection where one extreme of the phenotypic range is eliminated.

question: When members of a population attain sexual maturity at different times preventing inter-breeding, it is termed as __________ isolation. [MHT-CET 2017]
question_latex: When members of a population attain sexual maturity at different times preventing inter-breeding, it is termed as \_\_\_\_\_\_ isolation. [MHT-CET 2017]
solution: When members of a population attain sexual maturity at different times preventing inter-breeding, it is termed as Seasonal isolation.

question: Complete the analogy with respect to classification of mammals. Hyalobatidae : Gibbons : : Pongidae : _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Complete the analogy with respect to classification of mammals. Hyalobatidae : Gibbons :: Pongidae : \_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Pongidae includes semi-arboreal apes, Gorilla, Chimpanzee, and Orangutan.

question: The diagram represents Miller's experiment. Choose the correct combination of labelling.
question_latex: The diagram represents Miller's experiment. Choose the correct combination of labelling.
options: A. A-Electrodes B-NH3+H2+H2O+CH4 C-Cold water D-Vacuum E-U-trap
B. A-Electrodes B-NH4+H2+CO2+CH3 C-Hot water D-Vacuum E-U-trap
C. A-Electrodes B-NH3+H2O C-Steam D-U-trap E-Vacuum
D. A-Electrodes B-NH3+H2+H2O+CH4 C-Steam D-Vacuum E-U-trap
answer: A. A-Electrodes B-NH3+H2+H2O+CH4 C-Cold water D-Vacuum E-U-trap
solution: The correct combination of labelling is: A-Electrodes, $B - NH_3 + H_2 + H_2O + CH_4$, C-Cold water, D-Vacuum, E-U-trap.

question: Tarsiers show presence of claws on _____ toes of their hind limbs.
question_latex: Tarsiers show presence of claws on \_\_\_\_ toes of their hind limbs.
solution: Tarsiers show claws on their 2nd and 3rd toes of their hind limb. Lemurs have five divergent digits with nails, claws on second toes and use it for scratching and grooming.

question: Primitive man was originated during [MHT-CET 2006]
question_latex: Primitive man was originated during [MHT-CET 2006]
solution: Primitive man was originated during Pleistocene epoch.

question: Urey-Miller's experiment mixture had the following except
question_latex: Urey-Miller's experiment mixture had the following except
solution: In 1953, Stanley Miller and Harold Urey synthesized amino acids by passing an electric discharge in a mixture of methane ($CH_4$), ammonia ($NH_3$), hydrogen ($H_2$) and water vapours ($H_2O$). Carbon dioxide ($CO_2$) was not present in the Urey-Miller experiment mixture.

question:
The correct match of columns A, B and C is [MHT-CET 2016]
Column A: i) Coenozoic, ii) Palaeozoic, iii) Mesozoic, iv) Proterozoic
Column B: 2-65 mya, 500-165 mya, 135-225 mya, 350-500 mya
Column C: Origin of vertebrates, Rise of egg laying mammals, Reptiles dominant, Trilobites dominant
question_latex: The correct match of columns A, B and C is [MHT-CET 2016]
solution: The correct match is option iii: Mesozoic era (135-225 mya) with Reptiles dominant.

question: Earth is presumed to have originated about _____ billion years ago.
question_latex: Earth is presumed to have originated about \_\_\_\_ billion years ago.
solution: A single huge titanic explosion originated the universe 20 billion years ago. Earth is one of the planets in the solar system presumed to be originated about 4.6 billion years ago.

question: Which animal was very conspicuously visible on the tree trunks after industrial revolution (1845) in Great Britain?
question_latex: Which animal was very conspicuously visible on the tree trunks after industrial revolution (1845) in Great Britain?
solution: Darwin studied on the Biston betularia (Black pepper moth) seen on tree trunks of England forests (1845) showing two varieties. Natural selection plays an important role in evolution.

question: Sewall Wright effect is _______ [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Sewall Wright effect is \_\_\_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2020]
options: A. Transfer of genes between populations
B. Exchange of genetic material between communities
C. Changes in chemical makeup of a gene
D. Any alteration in allele frequency of a natural population by pure chance
answer: D. Any alteration in allele frequency of a natural population by pure chance
solution: Sewall Wright effect refers to cumulative changes in gene frequencies that arise from random birth, death, segregation purely by chance.

question: Neo-Darwinism is [MHT-CET 2004]
question_latex: Neo-Darwinism is [MHT-CET 2004]
solution: Neo-Darwinism has emerged out as the modern synthetic theory of evolution. It was designated by Huxley (1942). Neo-Darwinism is a refinement of original theory of natural selection to remove objections. According to this, both mutations and natural selection are responsible for evolution.

question: Which one of the following is NOT a character of disruptive natural selection? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Which one of the following is NOT a character of disruptive natural selection? [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. It tends to favor the intermediate forms
B. More number of individuals acquire peripheral value at ends of distribution curve
D. Two peaks are formed in distribution of traits
answer: A. It tends to favor the intermediate forms
solution: In disruptive selection, nature selects extreme phenotypes and eliminates the intermediate. Hence two peaks are formed in distribution of traits.

question: Which of the following is the most primitive ancestor of man?
question_latex: Which of the following is the most primitive ancestor of man?
solution: In the given options, Ramapithecus is the most primitive ancestor of man. Edward Lewis (1932) obtained fossil of Ramapithecus from Pliocene rocks of Shivalik hills of India. Ramapithecus survived about 14-15 million years ago during late Miocene to Pliocene and became extinct about 7-8 million years back.

question: Earthquakes may cause elimination of a particular allele leading to change in its frequency of a population. This is an example of [2022]
question_latex: Earthquakes may cause elimination of a particular allele leading to change in its frequency of a population. This is an example of [2022]
solution: Earthquakes causing elimination of a particular allele leading to random change in allele frequency in a population is an example of genetic drift.

question: Which of the following experiment suggested that simplest living organisms could not have originated spontaneously from non-living matter?
question_latex: Which of the following experiment suggested that simplest living organisms could not have originated spontaneously from non-living matter?
options: A. Microbes did not appear in stored meat
B. Larvae could appear in decaying organic matter
C. Microbes appeared from unsterilized organic matter
D. Meat was not spoiled, when heated and kept sealed in a vessel
answer: D. Meat was not spoiled, when heated and kept sealed in a vessel
solution: Meat was not spoiled when heated and kept sealed in a vessel. This experiment suggested that simplest living organisms could not have originated spontaneously from non-living matter.

question: Hardy Weinberg equilibrium is known to be affected by gene flow, genetic drift, mutation, genetic recombination and
question_latex: Hardy Weinberg equilibrium is known to be affected by gene flow, genetic drift, mutation, genetic recombination and
solution: The Hardy Weinberg law states that the gene and genotypic frequencies in a Mendelian population remain constant generation after generation if there is no selection, mutation, migration or random drift.

question: Total number of incisors, canines, premolars and molars in human dentition is respectively. _____
question_latex: Total number of incisors, canines, premolars and molars in human dentition is respectively.
solution: Human dental formula is I2/2, C1/1, PM2/2, M3/3 in each half jaw. So it becomes $8 + 4 + 8 + 12 = 32$ number of teeth.

question: In a population with two alleles B and b having frequency 0.7 and 0.3 respectively in Hardy Weinberg equilibrium. How many individuals are heterozygous in a sample of 250? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: In a population with two alleles $B$ and $b$ having frequency 0.7 and 0.3 respectively in Hardy Weinberg equilibrium. How many individuals are heterozygous in a sample of 250? [MHT-CET 2024]
solution:
Heterozygous $Bb = 2pq$
Here, $q = b = 0.7$, $p = B = 0.3$
$2pq = 2 \times 0.3 \times 0.7 = 0.42$
$= 0.42 \times 250 = 105$

question: The biological processes like cleavage, blastulation and gastrulation of all vertebrates are similar. It suggests __________ evidence. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: The biological processes like cleavage, blastulation and gastrulation of all vertebrates are similar. It suggests \_\_\_\_\_\_ evidence. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Ernst Haeckel proposed recapitulation theory which says that stages of development of animal embryo are same as other animals. As evidence he showed that stages of cleavage, blastulation and gastrulation are same in all vertebrates.

question: Cro-Magnon was
question_latex: Cro-Magnon was
solution: Cro-Magnon was the direct ancestor of the living modern man. It was omnivorous with 1680 cc cranial capacity.

question: Which set includes only analogous organs?
question_latex: Which set includes only analogous organs?
options: A. Wings of butterfly, housefly and bat
B. Hindlegs of horse, grasshopper and bat
C. Wings of butterfly and wingspread of bat and birds
D. Mandibles of cockroach, mosquito and honey bee
answer: C. Wings of butterfly and wingspread of bat and birds
solution: The organs which have similar function but different in their structure and origin are called analogous organs, e.g., wings of butterfly and wingspread of bat and birds.

question: Evolution is [MHT-CET 2007]
question_latex: Evolution is [MHT-CET 2007]
options: A. Development of DNA from nucleotides
B. Development of organism through time
C. Development of a cell from chemicals
answer: B. Development of organism through time
solution: Evolution is the event of changes through which an organism is descended from ancestor through time.

question:
Match Column I with Column II. [MHT-CET 2023]
(a) Australopithecus, (b) Homo habilis, (c) Homo sapiens, (d) Neanderthal man
(i) Advanced prehistoric man, (ii) Man with ape brain, (iii) Handy man, (iv) Modern man
question_latex: Match Column I with Column II. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Australopithecus - Man with ape brain; Homo habilis - Handy man; Homo sapiens - Modern man; Neanderthal man - Advanced prehistoric man.

question: Vestigial organ in human being is
question_latex: Vestigial organ in human being is
solution: Wisdom teeth are third molars of our dentition. Being useless, these are poorly developed and vestigial.

question: Which one of the following is INCORRECT with respect to genetic drift? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which one of the following is INCORRECT with respect to genetic drift? [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Effect of genetic drift has no significance in small populations
B. It is any alteration in allele frequency
C. It occurs in a population by pure chance
D. They are random or directionless
answer: A. Effect of genetic drift has no significance in small populations
solution: Effect of genetic drift has great significance in small populations. It is random fluctuation in allele frequency occurring in natural population by pure chance.

question: What was the most significant trend in the evolution of modern man (Homo sapiens) from his ancestors?
question_latex: What was the most significant trend in the evolution of modern man ($Homo\ sapiens$) from his ancestors?
solution: The first human-like being was the hominid called Homo habilis with brain capacities between 650-800cc. Homo erectus had a large brain. The Neanderthal man had a brain size of 1400cc. During ice age between 75,000-10,000 years back, modern Homo sapiens arose. The most significant trend was increasing brain capacity.

question: Jurassic period of the Mesozoic era is characterized by
question_latex: Jurassic period of the Mesozoic era is characterized by
options: A. Gymnosperms are dominant plants and first birds appear
B. Radiation of reptiles and origin of mammals like reptiles
C. Dinosaurs become extinct and angiosperms appear
D. Flowering plants and first dinosaurs appear
answer: A. Gymnosperms are dominant plants and first birds appear
solution: Jurassic period is the second geological period of Mesozoic era. In this period, gymnosperms were dominant, reptiles dominated the vertebrates, and the first primitive bird Archaeopteryx also made its appearance.

question: Which of the following is also called prehistoric man? [MHT-CET 2019]
question_latex: Which of the following is also called prehistoric man? [MHT-CET 2019]
solution: Homo habilis is also called 'Handy man' and is considered prehistoric man.

question: S. L. Miller's closed flask contained
question_latex: S. L. Miller's closed flask contained
solution: Urey and Stanley Miller's experiment in 1953 created an atmosphere containing hydrogen ($H_2$), ammonia ($NH_3$), methane ($CH_4$) and water vapours ($H_2O$) in one large flask. The ratio of methane, ammonia and hydrogen in large flask was 2:1:2.

question: In a random mating population in equilibrium, which of the following brings about a change in gene frequency in non-directional manner?
question_latex: In a random mating population in equilibrium, which of the following brings about a change in gene frequency in non-directional manner?
solution: Given certain conditions, the allele frequencies remain constant from generation to generation. However, many evolutionary changes usually occur following the appearance of new alleles through mutation, which brings about non-directional change in gene frequency.

question: Origin of life occurred in
question_latex: Origin of life occurred in
solution: The first living form is named as protocell or eobiont or protobiont, which evolved into prokaryotic cell. These were originated about 3900-3500 million years ago, during Precambrian era.

question: First mammal occurred in which era/period?
question_latex: First mammal occurred in which era/period?
solution: The Triassic period of Mesozoic era saw the primitive amphibians become extinct. An important event that took place late in Triassic period was the first appearance of mammals, which retained egg laying habit.

question: Mutation occurring within a single gene is called ........ [2022]
question_latex: Mutation occurring within a single gene is called ........ [2022]
solution: Point mutation is a mutation that occurs within a single gene involving change in a single base pair.

question: Wings of birds and wings of flies perform similar functions so they are examples of
question_latex: Wings of birds and wings of flies perform similar functions so they are examples of
solution: Organs which perform similar functions but having different origin and structure are called analogous organs. Wings of birds and wings of insects are analogous organs. Analogous structures are the result of convergent evolution.

question: Which fossil man has been known from Shivalik hills in India? [MHT-CET 2003]
question_latex: Which fossil man has been known from Shivalik hills in India? [MHT-CET 2003]
solution: Fossil of Shivapithecus was reported from Shivalik hills (India) from the rocks of Miocene epoch (about 20-25 million years ago).

question: Related species which are reproductively isolated but morphologically similar are called [MHT-CET 2011]
question_latex: Related species which are reproductively isolated but morphologically similar are called [MHT-CET 2011]
solution: Related species which are reproductively isolated but morphologically similar are called sibling species. Allopatric species have exclusive areas of geographical distribution. Sympatric species have overlapping areas of geographical distribution.

question: Which of the following pairs is correct? (identifying the non-vestigial organ)
question_latex: Which of the following pairs is correct? (identifying the non-vestigial organ)
solution: All those are vestigial organs which are now functionless, but flipper of seal is a functional organ and helps to swim. Hence, flipper of seal is not a vestigial organ.

question: Long tail, elongated tarsals and binocular vision are seen in ____
question_latex: Long tail, elongated tarsals and binocular vision are seen in \_\_\_\_
solution: Tarsiers are earliest primates belonging to Prosimians with long tail, elongated tarsals and binocular vision. Tarsiers are more closely related to Lemurs as they both belong to sub order Prosimii.

question: Hugo de Vries proposed 'Mutation theory' by studying the plant ________ [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Hugo de Vries proposed 'Mutation theory' by studying the plant \_\_\_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Hugo de Vries gave Mutation theory by studying plant Oenothera lamarkiana. Mirabilis jalpa is used for incomplete dominance experiment, Pisum sativum was used by Mendel in his experiments.

question:
Match the column I with column II regarding cranial capacities of human ancestors. [MHT-CET 2024]
(a) Australopithecus, (b) Homo erectus, (c) Neanderthal man, (d) Homo habilis
(I) 650-800 cc, (II) 450 to 600 cc, (III) 900 CC, (IV) 1400 CC
question_latex: Match the column I with column II regarding cranial capacities of human ancestors. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Australopithecus: 900 cc, Homo erectus: 450-600 cc, Neanderthal man: 650-800 cc, Homo habilis: 1400 cc. (Matching with stages of human evolution and their cranial capacity.)

question: Heavily built, short prehistoric man having cranial capacity of about 1450 cc is __________ [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: Heavily built, short prehistoric man having cranial capacity of about 1450 cc is \_\_\_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2018]
solution: Homo neanderthalensis was a heavily built, short prehistoric man having cranial capacity of about 1450 cc.

question: Lemurs and Tarsiers are included in sub order _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Lemurs and Tarsiers are included in sub order \_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Order: Primate, Sub order: Prosimian includes Lemurs and Tarsiers.

question: Hardy Weinberg principle explains
question_latex: Hardy Weinberg principle explains
solution: According to Hardy Weinberg law of equilibrium, the relative frequencies of various kinds of genes in a large and randomly mating, sexual panmictic population tend to remain constant from generation to generation in the absence of mutation, selection and gene flow.

question: Under particular set of selection pressures, the birds and mammals evolve towards the most effective reproductive strategy to produce [2022]
question_latex: Under particular set of selection pressures, the birds and mammals evolve towards the most effective reproductive strategy to produce [2022]
options: A. large number of large sized offsprings
B. large number of small sized offsprings
C. small number of large sized offsprings
D. small number of small sized offsprings
answer: C. small number of large sized offsprings
solution: Under particular set of selection pressures, birds and mammals evolve towards the most effective reproductive strategy to produce small number of large sized offsprings with greater parental care.

question: Which of the following animals is not only a living fossil but also considered as connecting link?
question_latex: Which of the following animals is not only a living fossil but also considered as connecting link?
solution: Neopilina is a living fossil and also considered as connecting link between Annelida and Mollusca.

question: Primary source of allelic variation is
question_latex: Primary source of allelic variation is
options: A. Due to long periods of evolutionary changes
answer: A. Due to long periods of evolutionary changes
solution: Diversification in plant life appeared due to long periods of evolutionary changes. The evolutionary changes sequence is: Bryophyte → Pteridophytes → Gymnosperms → Angiosperms.

question: Vestigial organ in human being is
question_latex: Vestigial organ in human being is
solution: Vestigial organs are non-functional or imperfectly developed organs. In man, wisdom tooth (3rd molar) and canines (tearing) are also the vestigial structures.

question: Homologous organs and vestigial organs are example of _____ type of evidences in evolution.
question_latex: Homologous organs and vestigial organs are example of \_\_\_\_ type of evidences in evolution.
solution: Anatomical evidences include homologous organs showing divergent evolution, analogous organs showing convergent evolution, and vestigial organs which give evolutionary history; all these are used as evidences in evolution.

question: Reptiles were dominant during _____ period.
question_latex: Reptiles were dominant during \_\_\_\_ period.
solution: Reptiles were dominant during Jurassic period of Mesozoic era.

question: Cosmozoic theory was proposed by
question_latex: Cosmozoic theory was proposed by
solution: Cosmozoic theory or hypothesis of Panspermia was developed by Richter (1865) and then supported by Thomson, Helmhontz, Van Tieghem and others. According to this hypothesis, life comes from outer space in the form of spores of simple organisms.

question: First cell produced on earth is
question_latex: First cell produced on earth is
solution: Protobionts are aggregated forms of different types of molecules, formed abiotically in the primitive sea. These are considered first cells produced on earth because they have an internal environment that differs from their surroundings and also exhibit some signs of life such as metabolism and excitability.

question: Which of the following was not explained by the Darwinism?
question_latex: Which of the following was not explained by the Darwinism?
solution: Origin of new species by the struggle for existence and survival of the fittest due to natural selection was the main theme of Darwinism. Arrival of the fittest (i.e., production of individuals already adapted to environment) was not explained by the Darwinism.

question: Which of the following are analogous organs? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which of the following are analogous organs? [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Thorns of Bougainvillea and tendrils of Cucurbit
B. Eye of octopus and of mammals
D. Forelimbs of Horse and of Birds
answer: B. Eye of octopus and of mammals
solution: Eye of octopus and of mammals are structurally different but functionally the same, making them analogous organs.

question: Movement of genes into or out of a population can occur due to following EXCEPT [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Movement of genes into or out of a population can occur due to following EXCEPT [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Movement of segments of DNA in a population
B. Doubling of genes on a chromosome
answer: B. Doubling of genes on a chromosome
solution: Doubling of genes on a chromosome takes place during 'S' phase of cell cycle and does not represent movement of genes into or out of a population.

question: What kind of evidence suggested that man is more closely related with chimpanzee than with other hominoid apes?
question_latex: What kind of evidence suggested that man is more closely related with chimpanzee than with other hominoid apes?
options: A. Evidence from DNA of sex chromosome only
B. Comparison of chromosome morphology only
C. Evidence from fossil remains and the fossil mitochondrial DNA alone
D. Evidence from DNA extracted from sex chromosome, autosomes and mitochondria
answer: D. Evidence from DNA extracted from sex chromosome, autosomes and mitochondria
solution: Chimpanzee is more closely related to man than other hominoids. It is evidenced by chromosome banding pattern, DNA extracted from sex chromosomes, autosomes and mitochondria.

question: The rise of 1st primates occurred in __________ epoch [MHT-CET 2014]
question_latex: The rise of 1st primates occurred in \_\_\_\_\_\_ epoch [MHT-CET 2014]
solution: The rise of 1st primates occurred in Palaeocene epoch.

question: Evolutionary history of an organism is known as
question_latex: Evolutionary history of an organism is known as
solution: Phylogeny (Gr. phylon=tribe or race; geneia=origin) is the origin and diversification of any taxon or the evolutionary history of its origin and diversification. It is usually represented as a diagrammatic phylogenetic tree (dendrogram).

question: The proportion of an allele in the gene pool, to the total number of alleles at a given locus, is called ____. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The proportion of an allele in the gene pool, to the total number of alleles at a given locus, is called \_\_\_\_. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The proportion of an allele in the gene pool, to the total number of alleles at a given locus, is called gene frequency.

question: Competition plays a critical role in __________ [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Competition plays a critical role in \_\_\_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Natural selection plays a major role in evolution. Every organism undergoes maximum reproduction creating competition among them for basic necessities. In this competition, the fittest survive, and natural selection plays a role in evolution.

question: Non living pair of colloidal aggregations of lipids and proteinoids are ____ and ____ respectively. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Non living pair of colloidal aggregations of lipids and proteinoids are \_\_\_\_ and \_\_\_\_ respectively. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Non living pair of colloidal aggregations of lipids and proteinoids are coacervates and microspheres respectively.

question: Select the correct match
question_latex: Select the correct match
options: A. Archaeopteryx - connecting link between birds and reptiles
B. Seymouria - Missing link between fish and amphibian
C. Australopithecus - Connecting link between amphibians and reptiles
D. Ichthyostega - Connecting link between ape and man
answer: A. Archaeopteryx - connecting link between birds and reptiles
solution: Archaeopteryx is the connecting link between birds and reptiles. Australopithecus is connecting link between apes and man. Seymouria is connecting link between amphibians and reptiles. Ichthyostega is connecting link between terrestrial vertebrates and fish.

question: The theory of random genetic drift was proposed by
question_latex: The theory of random genetic drift was proposed by
solution: The theory of genetic drift was proposed by geneticist Sewall Wright in 1930. It is also called Sewall Wright effect or scattering of variability. It refers to the random fluctuation in the gene frequencies in a small population generation after generation purely by chance.

question: Darwin proposed the theory of
question_latex: Darwin proposed the theory of
solution: Darwin proposed the theory of natural selection. According to which, the organisms provided with favourable variations would survive because they are fittest to face their surroundings, while the organisms unfit for surrounding variations would likely become extinct.

question: Which one of the following shows interspecific competition? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Which one of the following shows interspecific competition? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Interspecific competition is competition between two different species for obtaining same food. Lions and leopards are different species competing for the same prey.

question: Which one of the following was a special feature of Dryopithecus? [2022]
question_latex: Which one of the following was a special feature of Dryopithecus? [2022]
solution: Dryopithecus showed close similarity to chimpanzee. It was one of the early ancestors in the evolutionary lineage.

question: When two species of different genealogy come to resemble each other as a result of adaptation, the phenomenon is termed as
question_latex: When two species of different genealogy come to resemble each other as a result of adaptation, the phenomenon is termed as
solution: In convergent evolution, lineages show similar morphology under the influence of similar environmental factors.

question: Study of fossils is called [MHT-CET 2011]
question_latex: Study of fossils is called [MHT-CET 2011]
solution: Palaeontology is the study of fossils. Cytology is the study of cell structure and function. Herpetology is the study of reptiles and amphibians.

question: Which of the following in birds indicates their reptilian ancestry?
question_latex: Which of the following in birds indicates their reptilian ancestry?
options: C. Two special chambers crop and gizzards in their digestive tract
D. Egg with a calcareous shell
answer: D. Egg with a calcareous shell
solution: T. H. Huxley called birds glorified reptiles. Both the birds and reptiles lay the same type of eggs deposited outside water. The ovum is surrounded by albumen, an egg membrane and a thick hard calcareous shell, all secreted by special glands located in the walls of oviduct.

question: Formation of molecule _____ is considered as a landmark in the origin of life.
question_latex: Formation of molecule \_\_\_\_ is considered as a landmark in the origin of life.
solution: Formation of proteins is a landmark in origin of life as some of the proteins started acting like enzymes, regulate chemical reactions and accelerate the rate of chemical reaction.

question: An important evidence in favour of organic evolution is the occurrence of
question_latex: An important evidence in favour of organic evolution is the occurrence of
solution: Presence of homologous and vestigial organs are important evidences in favour of organic evolution. They show divergent evolution.

question: Molecular evolution and origin of life occurred in __________ Era. [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: Molecular evolution and origin of life occurred in \_\_\_\_\_\_ Era. [MHT-CET 2018]
solution: Molecular evolution and origin of life occurred in Archeozoic Era.

question: The number of base pairs present in the nucleoid of prokaryote like E. coli is ____ millions. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The number of base pairs present in the nucleoid of prokaryote like $E.\ coli$ is \_\_\_\_ millions. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The number of base pairs present in the nucleoid of prokaryote like E. coli is 4.6 millions with estimated genes 4400 according to genomics.

question: CORRECT sequence of stages of evolution of modern man is _____
question_latex: CORRECT sequence of stages of evolution of modern man is \_\_\_\_\_
options: A. Neanderthal man, Homo erectus, Australopithecus, Cro magnon man, Modern man
B. Homo erectus, Australopithecus, Neanderthal man, Cro magnon man, Modern man
C. Australopithecus, Homo erectus, Neanderthal man, Cro magnon man, Modern man
D. Cro magnon man, Australopithecus, Neanderthal man, Homo erectus, Modern man
answer: C. Australopithecus, Homo erectus, Neanderthal man, Cro magnon man, Modern man
solution: Correct sequence of stages of evolution in modern man is: Australopithecus → Homo erectus → Neanderthal man → Cro-magnon man → Modern man.

question: Phenomenon of industrial melanism demonstrates
question_latex: Phenomenon of industrial melanism demonstrates
solution: Phenomenon of industrial melanism demonstrates natural selection, e.g., occurrence of dark (melanic) form of insects in regions with high industrial pollution.

question: Lamarckism cannot explain
question_latex: Lamarckism cannot explain
options: A. Webbed toes in aquatic birds
B. Weak muscles in the son of a wrestler
C. Long narrow and limbless body of snakes
answer: B. Weak muscles in the son of a wrestler
solution: Lamarckian theory is also known as theory of inheritance of acquired characters or theory of use and disuse of organs. This theory cannot explain the reason of weak muscles in the son of a wrestler as acquired muscular strength is not inherited.

question: Thorns of Bougainvillea and tendrils of Cucurbita are examples of
question_latex: Thorns of $Bougainvillea$ and tendrils of $Cucurbita$ are examples of
solution: Thorns of Bougainvillea and tendrils of Cucurbita are homologous organs. These are modified branches and are axillary in position. Axillary branches in Bougainvillea are modified into thorns and in Cucurbita into tendrils for climbing.

question: Identify the correct sequence of stages in evolution of modern man/Homo sapiens
question_latex: Identify the correct sequence of stages in evolution of modern man/$Homo\ sapiens$
options: A. Australopithecus, Neanderthal man, Cromagnon man, Homo erectus and Modern man
B. Australopithecus, Homo erectus, Neanderthal man, Cromagnon man and Modern man
C. Homo erectus, Neanderthal man, Australopithecus, Cromagnon man and Modern man
D. Homo erectus, Australopithecus, Neanderthal man, Cromagnon man and Modern man
answer: B. Australopithecus, Homo erectus, Neanderthal man, Cromagnon man and Modern man
solution: The correct sequence of stages in evolution of modern man (Homo sapiens) is: Australopithecus → Homo erectus → Neanderthal man → Cro-magnon man → Modern man.

question: The Coenozoic era is often designated as
question_latex: The Coenozoic era is often designated as
solution: Coenozoic is regarded as the age of mammals. In this era, a variety of mammals like whale, bat and man appeared for the first time.

question: The reptiles, like dinosaurs were dominant in __________ period. [MHT-CET 2015]
question_latex: The reptiles, like dinosaurs were dominant in \_\_\_\_\_\_ period. [MHT-CET 2015]
solution: The reptiles, like dinosaurs were dominant in Jurassic period.

question: Homo-sapiens are most closely related to [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Homo-sapiens are most closely related to [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Homo-sapiens are most closely related to great apes like chimpanzee, gorilla and orangutan.

question: In which of the following events, there is no loss or gain in gene complement of the chromosome? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: In which of the following events, there is no loss or gain in gene complement of the chromosome? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: In Inversion there is no loss or gain in gene complement of the chromosome. A particular segment of chromosome is broken and gets reattached to the same chromosome in an inverted position due to 180° twist.

question: Point mutation is _____
question_latex: Point mutation is \_\_\_\_\_
options: A. Gradual, continuous change in genetic makeup
B. Sudden change in the chemical makeup of a gene
D. Changes in gene arrangement in chromosome
answer: B. Sudden change in the chemical makeup of a gene
solution: Mutations are sudden change in genetic makeup that is inheritable. Point mutation is sudden change in chemical makeup of a gene. Gradual and continuous change is evolution. Changes in gene arrangement on a chromosome mainly occur during crossing over.

question: Which postulate of Darwin's theory explains that the number of progeny tend to increase in geometric ratio? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Which postulate of Darwin's theory explains that the number of progeny tend to increase in geometric ratio? [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Overproduction (Prodigality of nature) is the natural tendency to produce more number of progeny in geometric ratio, for perpetuation of the species.

question: Louis Pasteur disproved the theory of ____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Louis Pasteur disproved the theory of \_\_\_\_ [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Louis Pasteur disproved the theory of abiogenesis which explains the formation of life from non-living material spontaneously.
